tasks.name: Project Scaffold
tasks.description:
# Task 01 — Project Scaffold (Vite + React + TypeScript + Capacitor)

## Objective
Stand up the foundational project skeleton for No Fault App 2.0: a Vite + React 18 + TypeScript web app, configured for Capacitor (iOS) wrapping later. Establish the folder structure, linting/formatting, and base tooling so every subsequent task builds on a consistent foundation.

## Context
This is a mobile-first internal employee app for The No Fault Group, rebuilding an existing app. Stack is React 18 + TypeScript + Vite (plain Vite — **NOT electron-vite**), with Capacitor for iOS packaging in a later task (T24). The data layer is Supabase (JS client v2, added in T02). Charts use Recharts (T18+). Server-side workflows/automation are DEFERRED — this phase builds UI + data layer with sensible defaults. This task only scaffolds; no feature code.

## Requirements
1. Initialize a Vite project using the `react-ts` template (React 18, TypeScript).
2. Install and configure Capacitor core + CLI (`@capacitor/core`, `@capacitor/cli`) and create `capacitor.config.ts` with `appId` (e.g., `com.thenofaultgroup.app2`), `appName` ("No Fault App"), and `webDir: 'dist'`. Do NOT add the iOS platform yet (deferred to T24).
3. Configure ESLint (TypeScript + React hooks rules) and Prettier; add `lint` and `format` npm scripts. Ensure they do not conflict.
4. Establish the folder structure under `src/`:
   - `src/components/` (shared UI), `src/features/` (feature modules), `src/pages/` (route screens), `src/lib/` (supabase client, utils), `src/hooks/`, `src/types/`, `src/theme/`, `src/assets/`, `src/routes/`.
5. Add a root `.gitignore` that **includes `node_modules/`**, `dist/`, `.env`, `.env.*`, `ios/`, `*.log`, and OS cruft (`.DS_Store`).
6. Add `npm` scripts: `dev`, `build`, `preview`, `lint`, `format`, `typecheck` (`tsc --noEmit`).
7. Configure `tsconfig.json` with strict mode on and a `@/` path alias to `src/`; mirror the alias in `vite.config.ts`.
8. Set viewport meta for mobile (`width=device-width, initial-scale=1, viewport-fit=cover`) in `index.html`; title "No Fault App".
9. Provide a minimal placeholder `App.tsx` rendering an app title, to prove the toolchain builds.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `npm install` succeeds; `npm run dev` serves the placeholder app.
- [ ] `npm run build` produces a `dist/` bundle with no errors.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass clean.
- [ ] `capacitor.config.ts` exists with correct appId/appName/webDir; iOS platform NOT added.
- [ ] `.gitignore` includes `node_modules/`.
- [ ] `@/` alias resolves in both TS and Vite.
- [ ] Folder structure created as specified.

## Dependencies
None (first task).

## Files Expected
- `package.json`, `package-lock.json`
- `vite.config.ts`, `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.node.json`
- `.eslintrc.cjs` (or `eslint.config.js`), `.prettierrc`
- `.gitignore`
- `capacitor.config.ts`
- `index.html`
- `src/App.tsx`, `src/main.tsx`, and the empty feature/folder structure

tasks.name: Supabase Client + Types
tasks.description:
# Task 02 — Supabase Client + `app2` Schema Typings & Env Config

## Objective
Wire up the Supabase JS v2 client targeting the `app2` schema, set up environment configuration, and generate/author TypeScript types for the `app2` tables plus the cross-schema reads. This becomes the single data-access foundation for all feature tasks.

## Context
Backend is Supabase (PostgreSQL). The app uses a dedicated `app2` schema (tables: `users`, `requests`, `request_comments`, `notifications`, `device_tokens`, `attorneys`, `tx_locations`, `insurance`) and performs cross-schema reads from `public.leads` and `public.employees`. Auth is Supabase magic-link + PIN (T04). The Supabase JS v2 client must be configured to default its `db.schema` to `app2`, with explicit `.schema('public')` calls for cross-schema reads. Server-side workflows are deferred — this is the read/write data layer only.

## Requirements
1. Install `@supabase/supabase-js` (v2).
2. Create `src/lib/supabase.ts` exporting a singleton client built from `import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL` and `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`, configured with `{ db: { schema: 'app2' }, auth: { persistSession: true, autoRefreshToken: true } }`.
3. Provide a helper (e.g., `publicSchema()`) returning `supabase.schema('public')` for `public.leads` / `public.employees` reads.
4. Add `.env.example` documenting required vars (`VITE_SUPABASE_URL`, `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`); never commit real secrets.
5. Author `src/types/database.ts` with TypeScript interfaces/types for the `app2` tables per SCHEMA.md:
   - `users` (auth_uid, email, employee_email ref, role enum, pin_hash, notification_prefs JSONB, special_view).
   - `requests` (id, type, status enum [11 values], title, description, submitter, office, approval flag, enhanced fields `case_after_jun2025`/`case_prior_jun2025`, timestamps).
   - `request_comments` (id, request_id, parent_id self-ref, author, body, created_at).
   - `notifications` (id, user_id, event_type enum [lead_assigned, lead_status, request_activity, approval_needed], payload, read_at, created_at).
   - `device_tokens` (id, user_id, token, platform, created_at).
   - `attorneys`, `tx_locations` (with coordinates geometry as lat/lng), `insurance` per SCHEMA.md key columns.
6. Define a `Role` union type: `'marketing_rep' | 'marketing_leader' | 'intake_specialist' | 'intake_supervisor' | 'admin'`.
7. Add lightweight typed query helpers in `src/lib/queries/` are optional here but export the table type names for reuse.
8. Document in a short `src/lib/README.md` how to regenerate types (`supabase gen types typescript`) when schema changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `supabase.ts` exports a configured singleton defaulting to `app2` schema.
- [ ] `publicSchema()` helper exists for cross-schema reads.
- [ ] `.env.example` lists required vars; no real keys committed.
- [ ] `database.ts` types compile and cover all 8 `app2` tables + the `Role` union.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` passes.

## Dependencies
- T01 (scaffold, env, folder structure).

## Files Expected
- `src/lib/supabase.ts`
- `src/types/database.ts`
- `.env.example`
- `src/lib/README.md`

tasks.name: Design System
tasks.description:
# Task 03 — Design System (Theme Tokens + Base Components)

## Objective
Build the mobile-first design system: theme tokens (colors, spacing, radii, typography) derived from the current app, plus a reusable base component library (Button, Input, Card, Modal/BottomSheet, Badge) and a core mobile layout primitive. This standardizes the look and feel for every screen.

## Context
React 18 + TypeScript + Vite, mobile-first (iPhone target via Capacitor). The current app's design system: primary accent teal/cyan (~#00BCD4), light gray card backgrounds (~#F5F5F5), light red/pink for alerts/negative, light green for positive, rounded corners, bold teal section headers, small uppercase teal labels (e.g., "SELECT PERIOD", "LEVEL"). Login/PIN screens are dark-themed; the rest of the app is light-themed. Many filters use a bottom-sheet modal pattern. No server-side logic here — pure presentation layer.

## Requirements
1. Create `src/theme/tokens.ts` with design tokens: color palette (teal primary + shades, success green, danger/pink, neutral grays, dark surface for auth screens), spacing scale, border radii, font sizes/weights, shadows.
2. Choose a styling approach (CSS variables + CSS Modules, or a lightweight CSS-in-JS); document the decision. Generate CSS custom properties from tokens for global use.
3. Add a global stylesheet with CSS reset/normalize, mobile-safe-area insets (`env(safe-area-inset-*)`), base font, and box-sizing.
4. Build base components under `src/components/ui/`:
   - `Button` — variants: `primary` (teal filled), `outline` (white outlined), `danger`, `ghost`; sizes; full-width option; leading icon slot; loading state.
   - `Input` — text/number/email; label; helper/error text; live-validation visual states (e.g., green "valid" banner for phone check).
   - `Card` — bordered, rounded, optional header (bold teal title); tappable variant with chevron.
   - `Modal` and `BottomSheet` — `BottomSheet` is the standard filter pattern (slide-up, "Clear all" + "Done" footer actions).
   - `Badge` — count badge (unread notifications), status pill, distance badge (teal).
   - `PillToggle` / `PillMultiSelect` — for Yes/No toggles and multi-select pills (e.g., Language).
   - `SectionHeader` — bold teal header with optional eye-icon show/hide toggle (collapsible sections).
5. Add an icon strategy (e.g., `lucide-react` or an `Icon` wrapper) for the icon set used across the app.
6. Provide a `Screen`/`Page` layout primitive (scrollable content area + safe-area padding + optional sticky header/footer).
7. Build a simple component gallery route (`/dev/components`, dev-only) demonstrating each component and variant.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Theme tokens defined and exposed as CSS variables.
- [ ] All listed base components implemented and typed, with variants.
- [ ] BottomSheet implements the "Clear all" / "Done" filter footer pattern.
- [ ] SectionHeader supports collapsible show/hide.
- [ ] Component gallery renders all components without errors on a mobile viewport.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T01 (scaffold).

## Files Expected
- `src/theme/tokens.ts`, `src/theme/global.css`
- `src/components/ui/*` (Button, Input, Card, Modal, BottomSheet, Badge, PillToggle, PillMultiSelect, SectionHeader, Icon)
- `src/components/layout/Screen.tsx`
- `src/pages/dev/ComponentGallery.tsx` (dev-only route)

tasks.name: Auth (Magic Link + PIN)
tasks.description:
# Task 04 — Auth (Magic Link + PIN Lock + Session + Route Guards)

## Objective
Implement authentication: Supabase magic-link email login, a 4-digit PIN setup/unlock screen (banking-app pattern), durable session persistence, and an auth context exposing the current user, role, and guard helpers. This gates the entire app.

## Context
Auth = Supabase Auth magic link + local PIN lock. User enters company email → Supabase sends magic link → on first successful auth the user sets a 4-digit PIN stored locally (hashed); subsequent app opens require the PIN while the session token persists (~30 days). Login is allowlist-controlled: the email must exist in `public.employees` AND `app2.users`. `app2.users` maps email → role. Login/PIN screens are dark-themed (305-NO-FAULT logo + "YOUR ACCIDENT TEAM" tagline). Role drives later route gating and feature visibility (see ROLE-ACCESS-MATRIX). No server-side workflow here; PIN is a local lock, not a server credential.

## Requirements
1. Build `LoginScreen` (`/login`, dark theme): logo, tagline, single email input, "LOG IN" button → call `supabase.auth.signInWithOtp({ email, options: { emailRedirectTo } })`. Handle deep-link/redirect return to complete the session.
2. On auth callback, verify the email exists in `app2.users` (allowlist). If not allowlisted, sign out and show a clear "not authorized" message.
3. Build `PinSetupScreen` and `PinUnlockScreen` (dark theme): 4-digit numeric entry. On first login (no `pin_hash` in `app2.users`), prompt setup (enter + confirm), hash client-side, store hash in `app2.users.pin_hash`. On subsequent opens with a valid session, require PIN unlock against the stored hash.
4. Implement an `AuthProvider` / `useAuth()` context exposing: `session`, `user` (joined `app2.users` row incl. `role`, `special_view`), `isLocked`, `signIn`, `signOut`, `unlockWithPin`, `setPin`.
5. Persist session via Supabase's storage; persist a local "unlocked" flag with sensible expiry so PIN is required after app cold-start/timeout but not on every navigation.
6. Implement route guards: `RequireAuth` (redirect to `/login` if no session), `RequireUnlock` (redirect to PIN screen if locked), and a `RequireRole(roles[])` helper (used by later tasks).
7. Provide graceful loading/splash state while session is resolving.
8. Store the PIN hash using a real hash (e.g., SHA-256 with a per-install salt) — never store the raw PIN.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Email login sends a magic link and completes a session on return.
- [ ] Non-allowlisted emails are rejected with a clear message and signed out.
- [ ] First login forces PIN setup; the hash is written to `app2.users`.
- [ ] Subsequent cold opens require PIN unlock; raw PIN is never persisted.
- [ ] `useAuth()` exposes session, user, role, lock state, and the listed actions.
- [ ] `RequireAuth`, `RequireUnlock`, and `RequireRole` guards work and redirect correctly.
- [ ] Session persists across reloads.

## Dependencies
- T01 (scaffold), T02 (supabase client + types), T03 (design system).

## Files Expected
- `src/features/auth/AuthProvider.tsx`, `src/features/auth/useAuth.ts`
- `src/pages/auth/LoginScreen.tsx`, `PinSetupScreen.tsx`, `PinUnlockScreen.tsx`
- `src/routes/guards.tsx` (RequireAuth, RequireUnlock, RequireRole)
- `src/lib/pin.ts` (hash/verify helpers)

tasks.name: App Shell + Routing
tasks.description:
# Task 05 — App Shell (Bottom Nav, Hamburger Menu, Routing, Role Gating)

## Objective
Build the persistent app shell: a fixed bottom navigation bar, a slide-out hamburger menu (sidebar), the React Router routing skeleton for all screens, and role-based route gating wired to the auth context. This is the navigation backbone every feature plugs into.

## Context
React 18 + React Router, mobile-first. The current app has a 3-tab persistent bottom nav: **Home**, **Scheduling**, **Mapper**. Per A5, the **Scheduling Center is REMOVED** — do NOT build it; the bottom nav is therefore **Home** and **Mapper** (2 tabs) unless a third destination is repurposed (default to 2). The hamburger menu (☰, top-left) slides in as a left overlay with header ("TheNoFaultApp" + bar-chart icon), menu items (1800 Directory, Request Portal, Atty & AFF Settings, Human Resources, Dashboards), and a user-profile footer (avatar, name, email, overflow → logout). Menu items vary by role (e.g., Atty & AFF Settings only for admin/intake_supervisor per matrix). Use placeholder route components for screens built in later tasks.

## Requirements
1. Set up React Router with a layout route wrapping authenticated screens in the shell (bottom nav + hamburger trigger). Auth/PIN screens render outside the shell.
2. Build `BottomNav` (fixed, safe-area aware): **Home** (`/home`) and **Mapper** (`/mapper`). Do not implement a Scheduling tab. Active-tab styling.
3. Build `HamburgerMenu` (left slide-out overlay with backdrop, X to close): header, menu items, and user footer.
   - Menu items + destinations: 1800 Directory (`/directory`), Request Portal (`/requests`), Atty & AFF Settings (`/settings/atty-aff`), Human Resources (`/hr`), Dashboards (`/dashboards`).
   - Gate **Atty & AFF Settings** to `admin` + `intake_supervisor` (per ROLE-ACCESS-MATRIX) — hide for other roles.
   - Footer: avatar, user name, truncated email, overflow menu with **Logout** (calls `signOut`).
4. Define the full route table (placeholders allowed) for: `/home`, `/mapper`, `/directory`, `/requests`, `/requests/new`, `/requests/:id`, `/hr`, `/dashboards`, `/profile`, `/notifications`, `/leads`, `/leads/:id`, `/leads/:id/edit`, `/settings/atty-aff`, plus drill-down routes (`/leads/pending-to-sign`, `/leads/ld-drops`, `/leads/new-today`, individual variants).
5. Apply `RequireAuth` + `RequireUnlock` to the shell layout; apply `RequireRole` to role-restricted routes using the matrix.
6. Provide a 404/not-found route and a basic error boundary.
7. Ensure the hamburger and bottom nav are accessible (focus trap on open menu, ESC to close, ARIA labels).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bottom nav shows Home + Mapper only; no Scheduling tab anywhere.
- [ ] Hamburger menu slides in/out, lists the 5 items, and gates Atty & AFF Settings by role.
- [ ] User footer shows identity and logs out via `signOut`.
- [ ] All listed routes resolve (placeholders ok) and are wrapped by auth/unlock guards.
- [ ] Role-restricted routes redirect/hide for unauthorized roles.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T01, T03 (design system), T04 (auth + guards).

## Files Expected
- `src/routes/AppRouter.tsx`, `src/routes/routes.ts`
- `src/components/layout/AppShell.tsx`, `BottomNav.tsx`, `HamburgerMenu.tsx`
- Placeholder page components under `src/pages/` for each route
- `src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx`, `src/pages/NotFound.tsx`

tasks.name: Home Screen
tasks.description:
# Task 06 — Home Screen Scaffold (Role-Aware Sections)

## Objective
Build the Home screen scaffold with role-aware section composition: hero banner, welcome text, call-center contact, the primary action button stack, and placeholders/containers for the Intake Dashboard card, chart sections, and Individual Performance. Sections render conditionally based on role so later tasks can fill them in.

## Context
React 18, mobile-first, light theme. Home (`/home`) is the post-login landing page; content varies by role (ROLE-ACCESS-MATRIX). It is a single-column vertical scroll with sticky bottom nav. Sections (from SPEC §3 Screen 03): A Hero banner, B Welcome text, C Call Center contact (786-708-2726, tap-to-call), D Primary action buttons, E Intake Dashboard card (org-wide counts; leader/admin-leaning), F Collapsible chart sections (Marketing/Intake/Atty Charts + Appointments & No Shows), G Individual Performance. This task builds the layout and the simple sections; the data-heavy sections (E, F, G) are stubbed for T08/T18–T22 and gated by role. Deferred workflows — use sensible defaults.

## Requirements
1. Build `HomePage` (`/home`) as a scrollable composition of section components.
2. **Section A — HeroBanner:** full-width decorative branding image/gradient (dark abstract, teal glowing edges).
3. **Section B — WelcomeText:** the intro message directing users to the hamburger menu and Request Portal.
4. **Section C — CallCenterContact:** red phone icon + "Dial: 1800 Call Center" + number `786-708-2726` + teal tap-to-call button using a `tel:` link.
5. **Section D — ActionButtons:** vertical stack. Include **+ New Lead** (teal filled primary → `/leads/new` route placeholder), Schedule Call Back, My Profile (`/profile`), Request (`/requests`), Dial (`tel:7867082726`). Lead Sheets / Lead Sheets v2 / Regional Rosters / Performance Roster Daily buttons render and route to placeholders (wired in T22). Use the matrix to show all to all by default unless a button is role-specific.
6. **Section E — IntakeDashboardCard (stub):** render a bordered card titled "Intake Dashboard" with placeholder rows (Active Leads, Pending to Sign, Individual Pending to Sign, Lock Down Drops, Individual LD Drops, New Leads Today) each with an arrow; wire counts in later tasks. Gate org-wide rows to leader/admin/intake roles.
7. **Section F — chart section containers (stubs):** collapsible `SectionHeader`s for Marketing Charts, Intake Charts, Atty Charts, Appointments & No Shows with correct default expand/collapse states (Marketing + Appointments expanded; Intake + Atty collapsed). Content filled in T19–T22.
8. **Section G — IndividualPerformance (stub):** container for the two card groups (always-visible + period-filtered); filled in a later task.
9. Compose sections per role: marketing_rep sees own-scoped sections; leaders/admin/intake see org-wide sections. Centralize the role→section visibility logic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Home renders all sections in order (A–G) on a mobile viewport.
- [ ] Tap-to-call works (Section C + Dial button) via `tel:` links.
- [ ] Action buttons route correctly (or to placeholders) with + New Lead styled as primary.
- [ ] Stub sections (E, F, G) show correct structure and default collapse states.
- [ ] Section visibility differs correctly between a rep and a leader/admin role.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T03 (design system), T04 (auth/role), T05 (shell/routes).

## Files Expected
- `src/pages/home/HomePage.tsx`
- `src/features/home/sections/HeroBanner.tsx`, `WelcomeText.tsx`, `CallCenterContact.tsx`, `ActionButtons.tsx`, `IntakeDashboardCard.tsx`, `ChartSections.tsx`, `IndividualPerformance.tsx`
- `src/features/home/homeVisibility.ts` (role→section logic)

tasks.name: User Profile + Notif Prefs
tasks.description:
# Task 07 — User Profile Screen + Notification Preferences

## Objective
Build the User Profile screen: identity display, timezone selection, external report links, collapsible Role/HR/Compensation sections, and a notification-preferences editor that writes to `app2.users.notification_prefs`. This gives each user a self-service profile and controls the opt-in events for the notification center.

## Context
React 18, mobile-first, light theme. Profile (`/profile`, Screen 11) is accessible to all roles. Current fields: a Zoom meeting link, a required timezone dropdown, four report links (Roster MTD, Logs 12mo, Logs v2, Perf Roster Daily — external Google Drive/Looker URLs), and three collapsible sections (Role, HR, Compensation) with eye-icon toggles. Profile/HR/role attributes come from `public.employees` (cross-schema read) joined to `app2.users`. Notification prefs are stored as JSONB in `app2.users.notification_prefs`. Notification events (opt-in per user, A6): lead_assigned, lead_status, request_activity, approval_needed. Known bugs to AVOID re-introducing: inverted eye icon on Compensation, wrong DOB/Start Date values, "Logs(v2)" formatting.

## Requirements
1. Build `ProfilePage` (`/profile`) reading the current user's `app2.users` row joined with their `public.employees` record (name, title, office, hierarchy, DOB, hire date).
2. Display identity header (avatar, name, email, role).
3. Timezone dropdown (required) — persist selection to `app2.users` (add/use a timezone field) or `notification_prefs`; default to America/New_York if unset.
4. Render the four report links as outline buttons opening external URLs in a new tab/in-app browser; format labels correctly (fix "Logs(v2)" → "Logs (v2)").
5. Three collapsible sections (Role, HR, Compensation) using `SectionHeader`; ensure the eye-icon toggle direction is consistent across ALL three (fix the inverted Compensation bug). Display the correct DOB and Start/Hire Date values from `public.employees` (validate formatting; avoid the wrong-value bug).
6. **Notification Preferences editor:** four toggles mapped to the opt-in events (New lead assigned, Lead status change, New request activity, Approval needed). Read/write `app2.users.notification_prefs` JSONB. Default all on for relevant roles; persist immediately on toggle with optimistic UI + error rollback.
7. Respect RLS: a user reads/updates only their own row.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Profile loads the user's joined employee + app2.users data.
- [ ] Timezone selection persists and reloads correctly.
- [ ] Four report links open correctly with corrected labels.
- [ ] All three collapsible sections toggle consistently (no inverted icon).
- [ ] DOB and hire/start date display correct values.
- [ ] Notification toggles read/write `notification_prefs` JSONB and persist.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T02 (supabase/types), T03 (design system), T04 (auth), T05 (routes).

## Files Expected
- `src/pages/profile/ProfilePage.tsx`
- `src/features/profile/NotificationPreferences.tsx`
- `src/features/profile/useProfile.ts`
- `src/lib/queries/profile.ts`

tasks.name: Notification Center
tasks.description:
# Task 08 — Notification Center (Bell, List, Unread Badges)

## Objective
Build the in-app notification center backed by `app2.notifications`: a bell icon with an unread-count badge, a notifications list screen with read/unread states, mark-as-read interactions, and deep-links to the relevant record. This is the Phase 1 notification mechanism (native APNs deferred).

## Context
React 18, mobile-first. Per A6, Phase 1 notifications are entirely in-app (no Apple infrastructure). Data source is `app2.notifications` with event types: `lead_assigned`, `lead_status`, `request_activity`, `approval_needed`; `read_at` tracks unread state, `payload` carries the target (e.g., lead_id or request_id). Users opt in per event via profile prefs (T07). RLS scopes notifications to the owning user. Native push and the `device_tokens` plumbing are built/used later (T24); this task is the in-app center only. There is no server-side workflow generating notifications yet — seed/insert helpers may be used for testing with sensible defaults.

## Requirements
1. Build a `NotificationBell` component (placed in the app header/shell) showing an unread-count `Badge` (count of rows where `read_at IS NULL` for the current user). Tapping navigates to `/notifications`.
2. Build `NotificationsPage` (`/notifications`): reverse-chronological list of the user's notifications, grouped or separated by read/unread; unread rows visually distinct.
3. Render per-event-type icon, title, and a human-readable message derived from `event_type` + `payload`; show relative timestamp.
4. Tapping a notification marks it read (`read_at = now()`) and deep-links to the target (lead detail `/leads/:id` or request detail `/requests/:id`) based on `payload`.
5. Provide "Mark all as read" action.
6. Implement a `useNotifications()` hook: query, unread count, mark-one-read, mark-all-read; subscribe to realtime inserts/updates via Supabase Realtime so the badge updates live (fallback to polling if realtime unavailable).
7. Respect notification preferences: optionally filter/label by the user's opted-in event types (display all stored rows, but the bell reflects unread regardless).
8. Provide a small dev/test seeding helper to insert sample notifications (since workflows are deferred).
9. Enforce RLS — only the current user's notifications are queried/updated.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bell shows accurate unread count and updates live (realtime or polling).
- [ ] Notifications list renders newest-first with clear read/unread distinction.
- [ ] Each notification shows correct icon/title/message per event type + relative time.
- [ ] Tapping marks read and deep-links to the correct lead/request.
- [ ] "Mark all as read" clears the unread badge.
- [ ] RLS prevents reading others' notifications.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T02 (supabase/types), T03 (design system), T05 (shell/routes), T07 (notification prefs — soft dependency).

## Files Expected
- `src/features/notifications/NotificationBell.tsx`
- `src/pages/notifications/NotificationsPage.tsx`
- `src/features/notifications/useNotifications.ts`
- `src/lib/queries/notifications.ts`
- `src/lib/dev/seedNotifications.ts` (test helper)

tasks.name: New Lead Form (5-step)
tasks.description:
# Task 09 — New Lead Form (5 Steps, Phone-Gated)

## Objective
Build the New Lead submission form — the principal action of the app. A single scrollable form with phone-validation gating, demographics, lead info, additional details, and media upload, plus the footer action panel (Hot Lead flag, Schedule Call Back, Submit). Submitting writes a new lead to `public.leads`.

## Context
React 18, mobile-first. New Lead (Screen 05, `/leads/new`) is universal — all roles can submit. Flow (SPEC §2): **Step 1 Phone Validation is the gating step** — a duplicate/validity check runs on input; a green "valid phone number" banner unlocks the rest; duplicates/invalid block progress (prevents duplicate pipeline entries). Step 2 Demographics (First/Last/Source). Step 3 Lead Info (boosted? toggle, where client lives, where accident occurred [state], passengers count). Step 4 Additional Details (Language multi-select pills: English/Spanish/Creole/Portuguese/Other; Notes). Step 5 Pictures & Videos (image upload + file picker). Footer: 🔥 Hot Lead toggle, 📅 Schedule Call Back, Submit. Leads live in `public.leads` (cross-schema write). Lead-submission automation/workflows are DEFERRED — write the row with sensible default status/fields. Enhancement E5: structured validation (no fabricated names, no DOB-in-name, no emoji/placeholder names).

## Requirements
1. Build `NewLeadPage` (`/leads/new`) as a single scrollable form with the 5 logical steps; steps 2–5 are visually disabled/locked until phone validation passes.
2. **Phone gating:** on phone input (debounced), run a duplicate check against `public.leads` (normalized phone) + format validity. Show a green success banner when valid+unique; show a clear blocking error for duplicate/invalid. Block submit until passed.
3. **Demographics:** First Name (required), Last Name (required), Source (optional free text). Apply E5 validation — reject empty/placeholder last names, emoji, and DOB-in-name patterns with inline errors.
4. **Lead Info:** "Is this a boosted lead?" Yes/No pill toggle (required); "Where does client live?" dropdown; "Where did the accident occur?" US-state dropdown with clear button (required); passengers numeric input.
5. **Additional Details:** Language multi-select pills (multiple allowed, teal highlight); Notes multi-line textarea.
6. **Media:** "Upload Images" button + file picker; upload to a Supabase Storage bucket and attach references to the lead. Handle multiple files and show progress.
7. **Footer action panel (dark teal):** Hot Lead toggle (flag), Schedule Call Back entry point (opens a date/time picker; persists a callback/appointment association — sensible default), and a full-width Submit button.
8. On Submit: insert into `public.leads` with mapped fields, default status = "New Lead", submitter = current user, hot-lead flag, language(s), passengers, source, accident state, residence, media refs, optional callback. Show success state and navigate to the new lead's detail or Home.
9. Use a form library or controlled state with a validation schema; disable Submit while invalid or in-flight.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Phone step gates the form: valid+unique shows green banner and unlocks; duplicate/invalid blocks.
- [ ] E5 validation rejects placeholder/emoji/DOB-in-name inputs with clear messages.
- [ ] All Step 2–5 fields render and validate per requirements; Language allows multiple selections.
- [ ] Image/file upload writes to storage and associates with the lead.
- [ ] Hot Lead flag and Schedule Call Back persist.
- [ ] Submit inserts a row into `public.leads` with default status "New Lead" and correct submitter, then confirms success.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T02 (supabase/types), T03 (design system), T04 (auth/user), T05 (routes).

## Files Expected
- `src/pages/leads/NewLeadPage.tsx`
- `src/features/leads/new/*` (PhoneCheck, Demographics, LeadInfo, AdditionalDetails, MediaUpload, FooterActions)
- `src/lib/queries/leads.ts` (insert + duplicate check)
- `src/lib/validation/lead.ts`
- `src/lib/constants/usStates.ts`

# Task 10 — Lead List / Active Leads with LEVEL Filter (Hierarchy-Aware)

## Objective
Build the Active Leads list screen with status grouping, search, a bottom-sheet filter, pagination, and the hierarchy-aware LEVEL filter that scopes which leads a user sees based on their position in the email-based org hierarchy. This is the core lead-browsing surface and the target of Home drill-downs.

## Context
React 18, mobile-first. Active Leads (Screen 12, `/leads`) drills down from the Intake Dashboard. It groups leads by 5 status groups: **CNA, Locked Down, New Lead, Pending, Rep Request Drop**; each group is paginated with search/filter. Lead data is `public.leads` (cross-schema read). Hierarchy is **email-based** in `public.employees` via `hierarchyTeam/Pod/Office/Region/Directorate`. The **LEVEL filter** (Individual/Team/POD/Office/Region) is cumulative — selecting a level aggregates all individuals within that scope; the default level is the highest the user's hierarchy position grants (rep→Team, office mgr→Office, regional→Region). Reps see OWN cases only; leaders see cascading hierarchy; admin/intake see broader scopes (ROLE-ACCESS-MATRIX). The bottom-sheet filter has 5 dimensions: Status, Intake Specialist, Office, POD, Team. Deferred workflows — read-only list with sensible defaults.

## Requirements
1. Build `LeadsPage` (`/leads`) listing leads grouped by the 5 status groups, each group with its own pagination and a count in the header.
2. Implement a **hierarchy resolver** (`src/lib/hierarchy.ts`): given the current user's email + `public.employees` row, compute the set of subordinate emails for each LEVEL (Team/POD/Office/Region/Directorate) by traversing the email-based hierarchy fields. Cache results.
3. Implement the **LEVEL filter** control (uppercase teal "LEVEL" label + dropdown: Individual, Team, POD, Office, Region). Default to the user's highest applicable level. Changing level re-scopes the query to the cumulative set of member emails.
4. Enforce role scope: marketing_rep is locked to Individual (own cases); leaders get levels up to their position; admin/intake get broad scope per matrix.
5. Search bar filters the current scope by client name / phone (debounced, server-side `ilike` where possible).
6. Bottom-sheet **Filter** (reuse T03 `BottomSheet`) with 5 dimensions (Status, Intake Specialist, Office, POD, Team), "Clear all" / "Done" footer; applies to the query.
7. Lead cards show: status icon, client name (submitter/agent in parentheses), phone, date/time, intake specialist, chevron → `/leads/:id`.
8. Server-side pagination (respect Supabase 1,000-row limit; paginate with `range()`); show page controls per group or a unified paginated list, matching the grouped design.
9. Make this page reusable for the Home drill-downs (Active Leads, Pending to Sign, LD Drops, New Leads Today) via props/filters (status preset, individual vs company-wide scope).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Leads render grouped by the 5 status groups with per-group counts and pagination.
- [ ] LEVEL filter scopes results cumulatively using the email-based hierarchy; default level matches the user's position.
- [ ] Marketing reps see only their own leads; leaders/admin see correct cascading scope.
- [ ] Search and the 5-dimension bottom-sheet filter both work and combine with LEVEL.
- [ ] Lead cards show all listed fields and navigate to lead detail.
- [ ] Pagination respects the 1,000-row query limit.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T02 (supabase/types), T03 (design system), T04 (auth/role), T05 (routes), T09 (leads query helpers — soft).

## Files Expected
- `src/pages/leads/LeadsPage.tsx`
- `src/features/leads/list/LeadCard.tsx`, `LevelFilter.tsx`, `LeadsFilterSheet.tsx`, `StatusGroup.tsx`
- `src/lib/hierarchy.ts`
- `src/lib/queries/leads.ts` (extend with scoped list + pagination)

# Task 11 — Lead Detail / Record View

## Objective
Build the Lead Detail view: a read-only summary of a single lead with status banner, contact actions (Call/Message/Email), an Intake-only "Edit Lead" entry point, and organized info cards (Lead Info, Demographics, Treatment & Appointment, Attorney, Accident Notes, Multimedia, Comments). This is the universal entry point from any drill-down chevron.

## Context
React 18, mobile-first. Lead Detail (Screen 23, `/leads/:id`) is accessible to all roles with role-gated actions. It reads a `public.leads` record plus related lookups (intake specialist, case manager/submitter from `public.employees`; attorney from `app2.attorneys`; tx location from `app2.tx_locations`). Layout (SPEC Screen 23): header card (status icon + label + lead name + submitted-by), contact row (phone + Call/Message/Email teal action icons), "Edit Lead" button ("Only Intake users" — gated to intake_specialist/intake_supervisor/admin → opens Intake Panel T12), Lead Info card (intake specialist, case manager, rep phone + Call Rep), Demographics, Treatment & Appointment, Attorney, Accident Notes, Multimedia, and a Comments thread. The richer 6-button action set (Save/Lock Down/Pending/Drop/Expand/CNA) belongs to the Individual Lead Record / Intake context (Screen 13/14) — surface those actions only for intake roles. Deferred workflows — actions update status with sensible defaults, no automated emails.

## Requirements
1. Build `LeadDetailPage` (`/leads/:id`) fetching the lead by id from `public.leads` plus joined lookups (intake specialist, submitter/case manager, attorney name from `app2.attorneys`, tx location from `app2.tx_locations`).
2. **Header card:** status icon (color-coded per status), status label (uppercase teal), lead name (bold), submitted-by line (agent + date/time).
3. **Contact row:** client phone (bold) + 3 teal action icons — Call (`tel:`), Message (`sms:`), Email (`mailto:`), using the client's contact info.
4. **Edit Lead button:** pencil + "Edit Lead" with subtitle "Only Intake users"; visible/enabled only for intake_specialist, intake_supervisor, admin; navigates to `/leads/:id/edit` (Intake Panel, T12).
5. **Info cards:** Lead Info (Intake Specialist, Case Manager, Rep's phone + full-width "Call Rep" button), Demographics (client name, phone, address), Treatment & Appointment (TX Location, TX Appointment, Is TeleMedicine), Attorney (attorney, signed-on date), Accident Notes (text). Render "No"/"Yes" correctly for booleans (avoid "false"/"true" display bug).
6. **Multimedia:** show uploaded images/files (thumbnails, tap to view).
7. **Comments thread (placeholder/light):** display existing lead comments if available; full threaded comments for requests are in T17 — keep lead comments read-display here unless data layer supports add.
8. For intake roles, surface quick status actions (Lock Down / Pending / Drop / CNA) that update `public.leads.status` with sensible defaults (the full editing flow is T12). Confirm-before-action for destructive Drop.
9. Handle loading, not-found, and error states.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lead detail loads by id with all joined lookups resolved (attorney, tx location, specialist, submitter).
- [ ] Status banner, lead name, and submitted-by render correctly.
- [ ] Call/Message/Email and Call Rep actions launch the correct native handlers.
- [ ] Edit Lead is gated to intake roles and routes to the Intake Panel.
- [ ] All info cards display correct values; booleans show Yes/No (not true/false).
- [ ] Multimedia renders uploaded media.
- [ ] Intake quick-status actions update status with confirmation on Drop.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T02 (supabase/types), T03 (design system), T04 (auth/role), T05 (routes), T10 (list entry points).

## Files Expected
- `src/pages/leads/LeadDetailPage.tsx`
- `src/features/leads/detail/*` (HeaderCard, ContactRow, LeadInfoCard, DemographicsCard, TreatmentCard, AttorneyCard, AccidentNotesCard, MultimediaCard, QuickActions)
- `src/lib/queries/leads.ts` (extend: fetch-by-id + joins, status update)

tasks.name: Intake Panel (Edit Lead)
tasks.description:
# Task 12 — Intake Panel (Edit Lead) with Progressive Disclosure

## Objective
Build the Intake Panel: a progressive-disclosure edit form for completing lead intake across 9 sections with gating logic, dropdowns sourced from `app2` reference tables, per-passenger sub-forms, and the Lock Down flow with status transitions. This is the core intake-specialist workspace.

## Context
React 18, mobile-first. Intake Panel (Screen 14, `/leads/:id/edit`) is intake-only (intake_specialist, intake_supervisor; admin). It edits a `public.leads` record through 9 sequential sections with gating (each section unlocks the next; Passengers gated until 1–8 complete). Sections: 1 Intake (accident date/type/location/fault/passengers; "Next" → Take Ownership → status=CNA), 2 Demographics (11 required fields incl. live phone validation, Is Minor, DOB, Language), 3 Insurance (PIP, At-fault, Uninsured — booleans must show Yes/No not false), 4 Injury & Damage (Property Damage 0–4 scale, Personal Injury 0–3 scale, Injuries multi-select), 5 Treatment & Appointment (TX Location dropdown, appt datetime, transportation + telemedicine checkboxes), 6 Attorney (Attorney dropdown, prior attorney, police report, DocuSign, signing date), 7 Questionnaire (13 Yes/No default No), 8 Passengers (per-passenger paginated, count from Step 1), 9 Accident Notes. Final: Save + Multimedia upload, then Lock Down (or Pending/Dropped/CNA). Dropdown sources: states (static 50), insurance (`app2.insurance`), TX Location (`app2.tx_locations` — affiliates + COR), Attorney (`app2.attorneys`); rating scales + injuries are static lists (SPEC §Screen 14). Deferred workflows — Lock Down updates status with sensible defaults; the LD email automation is NOT built (note transportation/telemedicine flags as "reflects on LD email" fields for later).

## Requirements
1. Build `IntakePanelPage` (`/leads/:id/edit`), gated to intake roles; load the existing lead and prefill all known fields.
2. Implement the 9 sections with progressive disclosure + gating; show a section progress indicator. Section 1 "Next" performs Take Ownership and sets status = CNA.
3. **Demographics:** 11 fields incl. live phone validation (reuse the T09 validator), Is Minor toggle, DOB, email, address/city/state/zip, Language multi-select.
4. **Insurance:** PIP (dropdown from `app2.insurance`), At-fault, Uninsured Motorist; render booleans as Yes/No.
5. **Injury & Damage:** Property Damage rating (Mild=1/Moderate=2/High=3/Major=4/N-A=0/No Visible=0), Personal Injury rating (Mild=1/Moderate=2/High=3/N-A=0), Injuries multi-select (Neck, Chest, Abdominal, Fractures, Shoulder, Knee, Facial, Lacerations, Foot/Ankle, Hand/Wrist, Soft Tissue, Eye, Seat Belt, Ear, Burn, Psychological, Arm, Leg, Deceased, Coma, Brain Damage).
6. **Treatment & Appointment:** TX Location dropdown (`app2.tx_locations`), appointment datetime, Requires Transportation + Is TeleMedicine checkboxes (label as LD-email-relevant).
7. **Attorney:** Attorney dropdown (`app2.attorneys`), Previous Attorney (Y/N req), Police Report Filed (Y/N req), Is DocuSign toggle, Signing Date.
8. **Questionnaire:** 13 Yes/No items defaulting to No (fix the "Questionnare" typo in labels).
9. **Passengers:** render `passengerCount` (from Step 1) paginated sub-forms, 9 fields each (First/Last/Is Minor/DOB/Phone live-validated/PIP req/TX Location/Appt Date/Personal Injury), with blocking validation + progress bar; gated until sections 1–8 are complete.
10. **Accident Notes** free text. **Multimedia** upload (reuse T09 uploader). **Save** persists all fields to `public.leads`.
11. **Lock Down flow:** when required fields complete, enable Lock Down (sets status → Locked Down, moves the record between live status groups in T10) plus alternatives Pending / Dropped / CNA. Confirm destructive actions.
12. Centralize reference-data loading (states, insurance, tx_locations, attorneys, rating scales, injuries) in hooks with caching.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Panel is intake-gated, prefills the lead, and enforces sequential section gating (Passengers gated until 1–8 done).
- [ ] All 9 sections render with correct fields, required-validation, and Yes/No boolean display.
- [ ] Dropdowns load from `app2.insurance`, `app2.tx_locations`, `app2.attorneys`; rating/injury lists match the spec.
- [ ] Section 1 "Next" sets status CNA (Take Ownership).
- [ ] Passenger sub-forms paginate per count with blocking validation.
- [ ] Save persists to `public.leads`; Lock Down/Pending/Dropped/CNA transition status with confirmation on destructive ones.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T02 (supabase/types), T03 (design system), T04 (auth/role), T09 (phone validation + uploader), T10 (status groups), T11 (detail entry point).

## Files Expected
- `src/pages/leads/IntakePanelPage.tsx`
- `src/features/leads/intake/sections/*` (Intake, Demographics, Insurance, InjuryDamage, Treatment, Attorney, Questionnaire, Passengers, AccidentNotes)
- `src/features/leads/intake/LockDownFlow.tsx`
- `src/lib/queries/reference.ts` (insurance, tx_locations, attorneys), `src/lib/constants/injuries.ts`, `ratings.ts`

# Task 13 — Employee Directory (1800 Directory)

## Objective
Build the 1800 Directory: a searchable, filterable, paginated employee directory grouped by department, with status tabs (Active/Termed/All/ICP). Data comes from `public.employees`. This is a reference/lookup screen available to all roles.

## Context
React 18, mobile-first. 1800 Directory (Screen 06, `/directory`) is accessible to all roles. Data source: `public.employees` (cross-schema read; ~2.3K rows). UI: status tabs (Active | Termed | All | ICP), a Home quick-nav pill, search bar + funnel filter, grouped by department (EXEC, Human Resources, Intake, Marketing, STAFF, Technology), 3+ pages of pagination. Row format: optional circular avatar + employee name + LEVEL value (LEVEL only populated for EXEC titles and Marketing rank tiers; blank for others). An asterisk (`*`) prefix denotes terminated employees in the All tab. Known data-quality issues: inconsistent name formats ("Last, First" vs "First Last"), username-style entries, service accounts ("Ads Nofault"), inconsistent photos. Known bug: "Termed" appears twice in filter options — do not duplicate. Deferred workflows — read-only directory.

## Requirements
1. Build `DirectoryPage` (`/directory`) reading `public.employees` with server-side pagination (respect the 1,000-row limit; use `range()`).
2. **Status tabs:** Active | Termed | All | ICP — filter by employment status (de-duplicate; ensure "Termed" appears once). "ICP" maps to its defined cohort (confirm field; default to a flag/department match).
3. Group results by department (EXEC, Human Resources, Intake, Marketing, STAFF, Technology, + any others present) with section headers and counts.
4. Row: optional avatar, employee name, and LEVEL value (render LEVEL only when populated). In the All tab, prefix terminated employees with `*`.
5. Search bar (debounced) filtering by name (handle both "Last, First" and "First Last"); funnel **Filter** bottom-sheet by department and office.
6. Exclude/flag service accounts gracefully (e.g., "Ads Nofault") — do not crash on malformed rows; tolerate missing photos/names.
7. Apply mandatory exclusions where relevant per project rules (Katiria Bonilla, xSTAFF) only if these appear and the directory is meant to mirror rep queries — otherwise show full directory; document the choice.
8. Tapping a row may open a lightweight employee profile sheet (name, title, department, office, hierarchy) — optional but preferred.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Directory loads from `public.employees` with working pagination.
- [ ] Active/Termed/All/ICP tabs filter correctly; "Termed" appears once.
- [ ] Results grouped by department with headers/counts.
- [ ] LEVEL shows only where populated; terminated employees marked with `*` in All.
- [ ] Search handles both name formats; department/office filter works.
- [ ] Malformed/service-account rows handled without errors.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T02 (supabase/types — public.employees), T03 (design system), T05 (routes).

## Files Expected
- `src/pages/directory/DirectoryPage.tsx`
- `src/features/directory/*` (StatusTabs, EmployeeRow, DirectoryFilterSheet, DepartmentGroup)
- `src/lib/queries/employees.ts`

tasks.name: Mapper (Locations)
tasks.description:
# Task 14 — Mapper (Treatment Locations, Map + Distance Sort)

## Objective
Build the Mapper: an interactive map of treatment locations from `app2.tx_locations` with a searchable list, plus an address-search mode that splits results into COR and AFF sections sorted nearest-to-farthest with teal distance badges. Intake uses this to find the closest treatment location to a customer's home.

## Context
React 18, mobile-first. Mapper (Screen 10, `/mapper`) is a bottom-nav tab (globe icon), used by intake (and available per matrix). Data: `app2.tx_locations` (PostGIS `geometry(Point,4326)` coordinates, GiST index; columns incl. `tx_location`, `tx_group`, `location_type` [AFF/COR], `address`, `state`, `coordinates`, `active_on_portal`). Default state: "All Treatment Locations" listed alphabetically with map pins; a list search filters by name (does NOT trigger address search); paginated. Address search: intaker enters a customer address → geocode → results reorganize into a **COR** section then an **AFF** section, each distance-sorted, with teal distance badges (e.g., "5.33 MILES"); no pagination on results (continuous scroll). Enhancement E4: de-emphasize/hide COR locations for non-FL addresses. Use a map library (Mapbox GL or MapLibre); document the choice + env key. Deferred workflows — read-only.

## Requirements
1. Build `MapperPage` (`/mapper`) with an interactive map (teal pins, zoom/pan) and a location list below.
2. **Default view:** load active `app2.tx_locations` (`active_on_portal = true`) alphabetically by name; render name + address; show pins; paginate the list.
3. **List search bar:** filters the loaded list by name only (client-side or server `ilike`); must NOT trigger the address proximity search.
4. **Address search bar (top, "Address:"):** on submit, geocode the customer address to lat/lng (via the chosen map provider's geocoder); compute distance to each location (PostGIS server-side via RPC `ST_Distance`/`ST_DWithin`, or client haversine fallback).
5. **Results layout:** two sections — **COR** first (distance-sorted), then **AFF** (distance-sorted); each row shows a teal distance badge (miles, 2 decimals), name, address. No pagination on results.
6. **E4 behavior:** when the searched address is non-Florida, hide or visually de-emphasize COR locations (config flag; default: collapse COR with a "show anyway" affordance).
7. Recenter/zoom the map to fit the searched address + nearest results; tapping a list row highlights its pin (and vice versa).
8. Handle data-quality issues gracefully (missing/incomplete addresses, bad coordinates) without crashing.
9. Document required env keys (e.g., `VITE_MAPBOX_TOKEN`) in `.env.example`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default view lists active locations alphabetically with map pins and pagination.
- [ ] List search filters by name without triggering address search.
- [ ] Address search geocodes and splits results into COR then AFF, each distance-sorted with teal mile badges, no pagination.
- [ ] Non-FL addresses de-emphasize/hide COR per E4.
- [ ] Map recenters on search; row↔pin highlighting works.
- [ ] Malformed location rows handled gracefully.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T02 (supabase/types — tx_locations), T03 (design system), T05 (routes/bottom nav).

## Files Expected
- `src/pages/mapper/MapperPage.tsx`
- `src/features/mapper/*` (MapView, LocationList, DistanceBadge, AddressSearch)
- `src/lib/queries/txLocations.ts` (+ distance RPC), `src/lib/geocode.ts`

tasks.name: Request Portal List
tasks.description:
# Task 15 — Request Portal List (Filters + Status Grouping + RBAC)

## Objective
Build the Request Portal list: requests grouped by status with live counts, a 4-dimension bottom-sheet filter, search, pagination within large sections, and role-based visibility (RBAC) that scopes which requests each user sees. This is the entry surface for the internal request system.

## Context
React 18, mobile-first. Request Portal (Screen 07, `/requests`) is available to all roles with RBAC controlling visibility. Data: `app2.requests`. There are **11 statuses** (Completed, Closed, Denied, Not Started, Completed - Confirmed, Needs Approval, In Progress, In Review, TBD - Needs more info, Technical Review, TBD - Nubia) and **21 request types** across departments (Audit, Finance ×4, HR ×7, Intake ×2, IT ×4, Retention). UI: header + "+ Submit Request" button (→ T16), requests grouped by status with counts and pagination in large sections; entry row = submitter avatar + name/timestamp (teal) + request type (bold) + description preview + chevron → detail (T17). **Filter bottom-sheet** has 4 dimensions: Request Type (21), Status (11), Office (15: ATL, CFL, CFL-ERU, CHI, JCK, NYC, SFL, SFL-ALR, SFL-GAB, SFL-KAT, SFL-KPS, SFL-TPA, TMP, TXS, xSTAFF), Approval (toggle). **RBAC:** Admin sees all departments; each department sees its own requests; marketing reps see only their own submissions; Finance visible to finance + internal marketing (e.g., Victor Gosa). Deferred workflows — no automated routing/approval emails.

## Requirements
1. Build `RequestPortalPage` (`/requests`) reading `app2.requests` with server-side pagination (respect 1,000-row limit, `range()`).
2. Render a top "+ Submit Request" button routing to `/requests/new` (T16) and an informational banner.
3. Group requests by status (11 statuses) with section headers + live counts; paginate large sections (e.g., Completed, Not Started) within their section.
4. Entry row: submitter avatar, submitter name + relative timestamp (teal), request type (bold), description preview (truncated), chevron → `/requests/:id`.
5. **Filter bottom-sheet** (reuse T03): Request Type (expandable 21-option checkbox list grouped by department), Status (11), Office (15-option list), Approval (toggle); "Clear all" / "Done" footer.
6. Search bar (debounced) over title/description.
7. **RBAC scoping** in the query layer:
   - admin → all requests.
   - department members → their department's requests.
   - marketing_rep → only own submissions (`submitter = current user`).
   - Finance department requests → visible to finance + designated internal marketing users (allowlist incl. Victor Gosa).
   Centralize this in a `requestVisibility` helper keyed off role + department + email.
8. Define shared enums/constants for the 11 statuses, 21 types (by department), and 15 offices for reuse by T16/T17.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Requests load grouped by the 11 statuses with counts and in-section pagination.
- [ ] Entry rows show avatar, name+timestamp, type, preview, chevron → detail.
- [ ] 4-dimension filter bottom-sheet works and combines with search.
- [ ] RBAC correctly scopes visibility (admin all; dept own; rep own; finance + internal-marketing rule).
- [ ] Shared status/type/office constants exported for reuse.
- [ ] Pagination respects the 1,000-row limit.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T02 (supabase/types — requests), T03 (design system), T04 (auth/role), T05 (routes).

## Files Expected
- `src/pages/requests/RequestPortalPage.tsx`
- `src/features/requests/*` (RequestRow, RequestFilterSheet, StatusSection)
- `src/features/requests/visibility.ts`
- `src/lib/constants/requests.ts` (statuses, types-by-dept, offices)
- `src/lib/queries/requests.ts`

tasks.name: Submit Request Flow
tasks.description:
# Task 16 — Submit Request Flow (Standard + Enhanced Forms)

## Objective
Build the Submit Request flow: a standard request form (type, title, description, image/file attachments) plus enhanced variants for Retention/Audit types that add case dropdowns and a bilingual warning. Submitting writes a new row to `app2.requests`.

## Context
React 18, mobile-first. Submit Request (`/requests/new`, Screen 07 form) is available to all roles. Standard form: dynamic title, description, image attachments, file picker, submit. **Retention/Audit** request types use an enhanced popup adding case dropdowns (the enhanced-form fields map to `app2.requests.case_after_jun2025` / `case_prior_jun2025`) plus a bilingual (EN/ES) warning. Request types (21) and statuses (11) and offices (15) come from the shared constants defined in T15. New requests default to an initial status (e.g., "Not Started") with sensible defaults — approval routing/automation is DEFERRED. Known bug B13: after submit the button turns green but the popup stays open and does not auto-navigate — FIX by auto-navigating to the new request or list on success.

## Requirements
1. Build `SubmitRequestPage` (`/requests/new`) using the shared request constants from T15.
2. **Request Type selector:** choose from the 21 types grouped by department; the chosen type drives a **dynamic title** and which form variant renders.
3. **Standard form fields:** title (dynamic/prefilled by type but editable), description (multiline, required), image attachments (multi-image upload to Supabase Storage), file picker (document upload). Show upload progress.
4. **Enhanced form (Retention + Audit types):** additionally render case dropdowns mapped to `case_after_jun2025` / `case_prior_jun2025` (sub-type selection per spec) and a bilingual EN/ES warning banner.
5. Validation: require type, title, description; enforce attachment size/type limits; disable Submit while invalid or in-flight.
6. On Submit: insert into `app2.requests` with type, title, description, attachment refs, submitter = current user, office (from user's `public.employees`), department (from type), default status "Not Started", approval flag default false, and enhanced case fields when applicable.
7. **Fix B13:** on success, close any popup and auto-navigate to the new request detail (`/requests/:id`) or back to the portal list with a success toast; do not leave the form stuck open.
8. Reuse the T09 media uploader where possible.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Type selector lists all 21 types by department and sets a dynamic title.
- [ ] Standard form validates and submits title/description/attachments.
- [ ] Retention/Audit types show case dropdowns + bilingual warning and persist the enhanced fields.
- [ ] Image and file uploads write to storage and attach to the request.
- [ ] Submit inserts into `app2.requests` with correct submitter/office/department/default status.
- [ ] On success the form auto-navigates (B13 fixed) with a confirmation.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T02 (supabase/types), T03 (design system), T04 (auth/user), T05 (routes), T09 (uploader), T15 (request constants + list).

## Files Expected
- `src/pages/requests/SubmitRequestPage.tsx`
- `src/features/requests/forms/*` (StandardRequestForm, EnhancedRequestForm, RequestTypePicker)
- `src/lib/queries/requests.ts` (extend: insert)

tasks.name: Request Detail + Comments
tasks.description:
# Task 17 — Request Detail + Threaded Comments + Approval Toggle

## Objective
Build the Request Detail screen: full request display, status management, an approval toggle for approver roles, and a threaded comment system (parent/child) backed by `app2.request_comments`. This completes the Request Portal loop and implements enhancement E4 (threaded comments).

## Context
React 18, mobile-first. Request Detail (`/requests/:id`) reads an `app2.requests` row plus its `app2.request_comments` (threaded via `parent_id` self-reference — enhancement E4). Visible per the same RBAC as the list (T15). Statuses (11) and types (21) from shared constants (T15). "Needs Approval" status + an Approval toggle exist; approver visibility is role/department-based. Comment/approval automation (notifications, routing) is DEFERRED — but inserting a comment or changing approval/status MAY create an in-app notification row (T08) for opted-in users as a sensible default. Render attachments uploaded at submit (T16).

## Requirements
1. Build `RequestDetailPage` (`/requests/:id`) fetching the request + submitter (from `public.employees`) + attachments; enforce RBAC visibility (reuse T15 `requestVisibility`).
2. **Header:** request type (bold), title, submitter + timestamp, current status badge, office/department.
3. **Body:** full description, enhanced case fields (when present), attachment thumbnails/links (tap to view/download).
4. **Status control:** allow authorized roles (admin, department owners, approvers) to change status across the 11 statuses; confirm destructive transitions (Denied/Closed). Persist to `app2.requests`.
5. **Approval toggle:** for approver roles, an Approve/Needs-Approval toggle (and Deny) that updates the approval flag + status; hidden for non-approvers.
6. **Threaded comments (E4):**
   - List comments from `app2.request_comments` ordered chronologically, nested by `parent_id` (render replies indented under parents).
   - Add a top-level comment and reply-to-comment composer; insert with `author = current user`, `request_id`, optional `parent_id`, body, timestamp.
   - Optimistic insert with rollback on error.
7. On comment add / status change / approval change, optionally insert an `app2.notifications` row (`request_activity` or `approval_needed`) for relevant opted-in users (sensible default; no email/push).
8. Handle loading/not-found/unauthorized states; respect RLS.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Request detail loads with header, body, enhanced fields, and attachments; RBAC enforced.
- [ ] Authorized roles can change status (with confirmation on destructive); persists correctly.
- [ ] Approval toggle/Deny shows only for approvers and updates approval + status.
- [ ] Threaded comments render nested by parent_id; users can add top-level and reply comments that persist.
- [ ] Comment/status/approval changes optionally create in-app notifications for opted-in users.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T02 (supabase/types — requests, request_comments), T03 (design system), T04 (auth/role), T08 (notifications — soft), T15 (visibility/constants), T16 (submit/attachments).

## Files Expected
- `src/pages/requests/RequestDetailPage.tsx`
- `src/features/requests/detail/*` (RequestHeader, StatusControl, ApprovalToggle, CommentThread, CommentComposer)
- `src/lib/queries/requestComments.ts`, extend `src/lib/queries/requests.ts` (status/approval update)

tasks.name: Chart Infrastructure
tasks.description:
# Task 18 — Chart Infrastructure (Recharts Wrappers + Supabase RPC Aggregations)

## Objective
Build the reusable chart infrastructure: Recharts-based donut/ring + data-table wrapper components matching the app's chart style, a standard "metric chart" composite (donut + sortable table), and the Supabase RPC aggregation layer that feeds them. Every dashboard task (T19–T22) builds on this.

## Context
React 18 + TypeScript, mobile-first. Per A4, charts are native React via **Recharts** (NOT Looker Studio iframes), fed by **Supabase RPC functions** for server-side aggregation (avoids the 1,000-row client limit). The standard chart composition (SPEC): a donut/ring with one segment per entity (rep/specialist/attorney), a center "Total" label + aggregate number, teal color gradient (dark teal for top values → light gray for low), and a data table below (header bar dark teal, two columns ENTITY | METRIC, sorted descending, scrollable, alternating row separators). Period filters are Today / MTD. This task delivers reusable primitives + the RPC contract; specific metrics are implemented in T19–T22. Mandatory exclusions (Katiria Bonilla, xSTAFF) must be applied in aggregation. Default date field = `idot` (Initial Date of Treatment).

## Requirements
1. Install and configure `recharts`.
2. Build `DonutChart` wrapper: accepts `{ label, value }[]` data, renders a ring with center Total (label + sum), teal gradient ordering by value (dark→light), responsive sizing for mobile, accessible labels.
3. Build `MetricTable`: two columns (entity name | metric value), dark-teal header, sorted descending, scrollable, alternating rows; configurable column headers.
4. Build `MetricChart` composite: title header + `DonutChart` + `MetricTable` for a single metric/period; loading + empty states. Optional E1 sub-group breakdown toggle (Individual / Team / Pod / Office) — provide the prop hook even if data wiring lands later.
5. Build a `PeriodToggle` (Today / MTD) and a generic `ChartSection` wrapper (collapsible `SectionHeader` + period toggle + children).
6. Define the **RPC aggregation contract**: author SQL function signatures (documented in `src/lib/charts/RPC.md`) for server-side counts grouped by entity over a period, e.g. `app2.chart_counts(metric text, period text, scope jsonb)` returning `{ entity, count }`. Apply mandatory exclusions and use `idot` as the default date basis.
7. Build a `useChartData(metric, period, scope)` hook calling the RPCs, with caching, loading/error states, and typed results.
8. Provide a color-scale utility mapping ranked values to the teal→gray gradient.
9. Add a dev gallery route showing the chart primitives with mock data.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DonutChart renders ring + center total with the teal→gray gradient, responsive on mobile.
- [ ] MetricTable renders sorted-descending two-column table with the dark-teal header style.
- [ ] MetricChart composes donut + table with loading/empty states and an E1 sub-group toggle prop.
- [ ] PeriodToggle and ChartSection wrappers work (collapsible + Today/MTD).
- [ ] RPC contract documented; `useChartData` hook calls RPCs, applies exclusions, uses `idot`.
- [ ] Dev gallery renders primitives with mock data.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T02 (supabase/types), T03 (design system), T05 (routes).

## Files Expected
- `src/features/charts/DonutChart.tsx`, `MetricTable.tsx`, `MetricChart.tsx`, `PeriodToggle.tsx`, `ChartSection.tsx`
- `src/features/charts/useChartData.ts`, `src/lib/charts/colorScale.ts`
- `src/lib/charts/RPC.md` (SQL function contract)
- `src/pages/dev/ChartGallery.tsx`

tasks.name: Marketing Charts
tasks.description:
# Task 19 — Marketing Charts (6 Metrics × 2 Periods, LEVEL Filter)

## Objective
Build the Marketing Charts section: 6 performance metrics across Today/MTD periods rendered as donut + table charts (by rep / Case Manager), with the hierarchy-aware LEVEL filter controlling aggregation scope. This is the primary rep-performance dashboard on Home (Section F).

## Context
React 18, mobile-first. Marketing Charts (Home Section F) use the T18 chart infrastructure. **6 metrics** (each Today + MTD = 12 chart views): New Leads (NLT/NLMTD), Locked Down (LDT/LDMTD), Came In (CIT/CIMTD), Signed (ST/SMTD), Dropped (DT/DMTD), Locked Down Dropped (LDDT/LDDMTD). Charts are "by Case Manager" (rep). The **LEVEL filter** (Individual/Team/POD/Office/Region) is cumulative and uses the email-based hierarchy from `public.employees` (reuse the T10 hierarchy resolver); default = the user's highest applicable level. Special-view users (Zeri, Christian, Tony, Willie, Karla, Jon, Andres, Humberto) override normal hierarchy scope — handle via `app2.users.special_view`. Access: marketing_rep (own scope), marketing_leader (cascading), admin (all) — per ROLE-ACCESS-MATRIX. Data via T18 RPCs over `public.leads`, date basis `idot`, mandatory exclusions applied. Deferred workflows — read-only aggregation.

## Requirements
1. Build `MarketingCharts` section (rendered in Home Section F), default expanded, using `ChartSection` + `PeriodToggle` (Today / MTD).
2. Implement the **6 metrics** as `MetricChart`s for the selected period: New Leads, Locked Down, Came In, Signed, Dropped, Locked Down Dropped — each "by Case Manager" with entity = rep.
3. Wire each metric to a T18 RPC that counts leads grouped by rep within the resolved scope, period, and metric definition:
   - Locked Down = both attorney + treatment assigned; Came In = patient attended treatment; Signed = signed; Dropped = dropped; Locked Down Dropped = locked down then dropped.
4. **LEVEL filter** control (uppercase teal "LEVEL"): Individual/Team/POD/Office/Region; default to the user's highest applicable level (reuse T10 hierarchy resolver to compute the cumulative member-email set per level).
5. **Special-view override:** if the user has `special_view` config, apply the configured scope instead of the standard hierarchy default (e.g., full-org view). Centralize special-view resolution.
6. **Role scoping:** marketing_rep limited to Individual; leaders limited to their position level and below; admin all levels.
7. Each chart shows the donut (center Total) + descending table (REP | METRIC). Honor the E1 sub-group toggle prop from T18 (Team/Pod/Office breakdown) where feasible.
8. Loading/empty/error states per chart; changing LEVEL or period re-queries.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Section renders 6 metric charts for the selected period (Today/MTD toggle works).
- [ ] Each metric aggregates correctly by rep via RPC using the right metric definition and `idot` basis.
- [ ] LEVEL filter re-scopes cumulatively using the email-based hierarchy; default matches the user's position.
- [ ] Special-view users get their overridden scope.
- [ ] Role scoping limits reps to Individual; leaders/admin get correct levels.
- [ ] Mandatory exclusions applied; loading/empty states present.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T10 (hierarchy resolver), T18 (chart infra + RPC contract), T06 (Home Section F container), T04 (auth/role/special_view).

## Files Expected
- `src/features/charts/marketing/MarketingCharts.tsx`
- `src/features/charts/marketing/marketingMetrics.ts` (metric defs)
- `src/lib/charts/specialView.ts`
- RPC additions documented in `src/lib/charts/RPC.md`

# Task 20 — Intake Charts (3 Metrics × 2 Periods)

## Objective
Build the Intake Charts section: 3 intake-specialist performance metrics across Today/MTD as donut + table charts, by Intake Specialist. This is the intake counterpart to Marketing Charts on Home (Section F).

## Context
React 18, mobile-first. Intake Charts (Home Section F) reuse the T18 chart infrastructure and the same donut+table format as Marketing Charts. **3 metrics** (each Today + MTD = 6 chart views), tracked **by Intake Specialist**: Came In (patient attended treatment), Locked Down (attorney + treatment assigned), Signed. Charts appear in a single scrollable list (Today first, then MTD) and the section defaults to collapsed ("Show"). Note: the current app's table header column says "OFFICE" but lists individual specialist names — use a correct "INTAKE SPECIALIST" column header (do not reproduce the mislabel). No LEVEL filter is confirmed for Intake Charts (omit unless role requires it). Data via T18 RPCs over `public.leads`, date basis `idot`, mandatory exclusions applied. Access per ROLE-ACCESS-MATRIX (intake-leaning; confirm same period behavior). Deferred workflows — read-only.

## Requirements
1. Build `IntakeCharts` section (Home Section F), default collapsed, using `ChartSection` + `PeriodToggle` (Today / MTD). If the source presents Today then MTD stacked rather than a toggle, support a stacked mode but default to the toggle for parity with Marketing.
2. Implement the **3 metrics** as `MetricChart`s for the selected period: Came In, Locked Down, Signed — entity = Intake Specialist.
3. Wire each to a T18 RPC counting leads grouped by intake specialist within the period and metric definition; apply mandatory exclusions and `idot` basis.
4. Use the correct table column header "INTAKE SPECIALIST | <METRIC>" (fix the "OFFICE" mislabel).
5. No LEVEL filter by default; if a role needs scoping, gate by role rather than adding the hierarchy selector.
6. Honor the E1 sub-group breakdown toggle prop where feasible.
7. Loading/empty/error states; changing period re-queries.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Section renders 3 metric charts (Came In, Locked Down, Signed) for the selected period; Today/MTD toggle works.
- [ ] Aggregation is by intake specialist via RPC with correct metric definitions and `idot` basis.
- [ ] Table header reads "INTAKE SPECIALIST" (mislabel fixed).
- [ ] Section defaults to collapsed; expands correctly.
- [ ] Mandatory exclusions applied; loading/empty states present.

tasks.description:
# Task 21 — Attorney Charts (9 Charts, by Attorney Firm)

## Objective
Build the Attorney Charts section: performance metrics aggregated **by attorney firm** (with location/state codes) rendered as donut + table charts, plus the ATTORNEY / NEW LEAD TODAY filter buttons. This is the attorney-performance dashboard on Home (Section F).

## Context
React 18, mobile-first. Atty Charts (Home Section F) reuse the T18 chart infrastructure and the donut+table format, but aggregate **by attorney firm** (external law firms), NOT by employee. Firms appear as separate entries per state/office with parenthetical codes (states like FL/NJ/NY, cities like TMP/ORL/JAC, designations like NS/WC/RO). Attorney reference data is `app2.attorneys`; counts come from `public.leads` joined to attorney. **~9 charts** across periods: New Leads MTD, Locked Down (Today/MTD), Came In Today, Signed (Today/MTD), Dropped Today, Lock Down Dropped Today (per SPEC list). Two teal **filter buttons** at the top: ATTORNEY and NEW LEAD TODAY. There is **no LEVEL filter** for Atty Charts. Lists can be long (40+ firms) and must scroll. Section defaults to collapsed. Data via T18 RPCs, `idot` basis, mandatory exclusions applied. Deferred workflows — read-only.

## Requirements
1. Build `AttyCharts` section (Home Section F), default collapsed, using `ChartSection`.
2. Implement the ~9 charts as `MetricChart`s with entity = attorney firm (display name = firm + parenthetical code from `app2.attorneys`):
   - New Leads MTD, Locked Down Today, Locked Down MTD, Came In Today, Signed Today, Signed MTD, Dropped Today, Lock Down Dropped Today (align exactly to the SPEC list; de-duplicate any repeated titles).
3. Wire each chart to a T18 RPC counting leads grouped by attorney (firm + state/office variant) for the metric + period; apply mandatory exclusions and `idot` basis. Handle the "No ATTY" / null-attorney edge case as its own row.
4. **Filter buttons:** ATTORNEY (filter/sort by firm) and NEW LEAD TODAY (filter to today's new leads). Implement both as teal toggle buttons affecting the displayed charts/data; document any behavior assumptions for the NEW LEAD TODAY button.
5. No LEVEL filter.
6. Tables must scroll smoothly for 40+ firm rows; donuts cap visible segments sensibly (group long tail) while the table shows all.
7. Loading/empty/error states.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Section renders the attorney charts by firm (with parenthetical codes) per the SPEC list, de-duplicated.
- [ ] Each chart aggregates correctly by attorney firm via RPC with `idot` basis and exclusions; "No ATTY" edge case handled.
- [ ] ATTORNEY and NEW LEAD TODAY filter buttons function and affect the data shown.
- [ ] No LEVEL filter is present.
- [ ] Long firm lists scroll; donut long-tail grouped while table is complete.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T18 (chart infra + RPC contract), T06 (Home Section F container), T02 (attorneys table), T04 (auth/role).

## Files Expected
- `src/features/charts/atty/AttyCharts.tsx`
- `src/features/charts/atty/attyMetrics.ts`, `AttyFilterButtons.tsx`
- RPC additions documented in `src/lib/charts/RPC.md`

tasks.description:
# Task 22 — Performance Roster / Lead Sheets (Per-Rep, Hierarchy-Scoped) + Individual Performance + Home Drill-downs

## Objective
Build the rep-performance roster surfaces and tie off the remaining Home data sections: the Regional/Performance Roster (per-rep metrics by office/region, hierarchy-scoped), Lead Sheets, the Home Individual Performance section (Section G), and the Home drill-down lists (Active Leads, Pending to Sign, LD Drops, New Leads Today, individual variants) plus the Appointments & No Shows list.

## Context
React 18, mobile-first. This task implements the native-React replacements for the former Looker Studio reports (A4: no Looker dependency) and completes Home data. **Regional Rosters / Performance Roster** (Screens 18/19): rep-level metrics — Leads Created, Goal, TTD, Converted, %Goal, weekly breakdown (W1–W5), Drop, title tier, hire date — grouped by office with summary headers, hierarchy-scoped via the email-based hierarchy (T10 resolver). **Lead Sheets** (Screens 16/17): per-rep client contact grid. **Home Section G — Individual Performance:** Group 1 always-visible cards (Active Leads green, Pending to Sign gray) + Group 2 period-filtered cards (Locked Down, Came In, Dropped) with Today/MTD toggle; cards tappable only when count > 0 → drill-down lists. **Home drill-downs** reuse the T10 `LeadsPage` with presets (Screens 12, 20, 21, 21b, 22). **Appointments & No Shows** (Home Section F): paginated client appointment list (NOT a chart). Data via T18 RPCs + `public.leads`/`public.employees`, `idot` basis, exclusions. Fix bugs: B3 "SHow"→"Show", B4 "intake Specialist", B6 "Droped"→"Dropped". Deferred workflows — read-only.

## Requirements
1. **Performance Roster** (`/roster` or via Home buttons → wire the Section D placeholder buttons): per-office sections with summary headers (DATE, LAST DAY, GOAL, TTD, CNV, %GOAL, #REPS, #Drop) and rep tables (#, NAME, LEADS CREATED, IND/BOOST/TEAM GOAL, TTD, CONVERTED, %GOAL color-coded, W1–W5, DROPPED, TITLE, DH). Hierarchy-scoped via T10 resolver; sorted by %Goal descending; include a Global Operations totals row. Provide PDF export (print-to-PDF acceptable).
2. **Lead Sheets** (`/lead-sheets`): per-rep client grid (Client Name, Director, Phone, Submitter, Tx Location, Created) + KPI cards (No Show, New Lead, Future, totals). Scope to the rep (or selected rep for leaders/admin).
3. Wire Home Section D buttons (Lead Sheets, Lead Sheets v2, Regional Rosters, Performance Roster Daily) to these native screens (replace external Looker links).
4. **Home Section G — Individual Performance:** implement Group 1 (Active Leads, Pending to Sign — always visible, color-coded, tappable when >0) and Group 2 (Locked Down, Came In, Dropped — Today/MTD toggle, tappable when >0). Tapping routes to the matching drill-down list.
5. **Home drill-downs:** configure `LeadsPage` (T10) presets for Active Leads (5 status groups), New Leads Today (Hot Leads zone + Submitted Today zone with sub-sections), Pending to Sign (company-wide + individual 21b), Lock Down Drops (company-wide + individual). Individual variants drop search/filter/pagination per spec.
6. **Appointments & No Shows** (Home Section F): paginated list of today's client appointments (icon, client name, date/time, chevron → lead detail); fix the "SHow" typo to "Show".
7. Apply bug fixes B3/B4/B6 in all relevant labels; ensure %Goal color tiers (green/yellow) render.
8. Use T18 RPCs for roster aggregations to bypass the 1,000-row limit; apply mandatory exclusions and `idot` basis.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Performance Roster renders per-office summaries + rep tables with all columns, %Goal color tiers, sorted by %Goal desc, with a Global Operations total and PDF export.
- [ ] Lead Sheets renders the per-rep client grid + KPI cards, correctly scoped.
- [ ] Home Section D roster/lead-sheet buttons route to these native screens (no Looker links).
- [ ] Individual Performance Section G shows both card groups with correct color coding and >0 tappability + drill-downs.
- [ ] Home drill-downs (Active Leads, New Leads Today zones, Pending to Sign, LD Drops + individual variants) work via LeadsPage presets.
- [ ] Appointments & No Shows list paginates and links to lead detail; "Show" typo fixed.
- [ ] Bugs B3/B4/B6 fixed; exclusions + `idot` applied; pagination respects 1,000-row limit.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T10 (LeadsPage + hierarchy), T11 (lead detail), T18 (chart/RPC infra), T19/T20 (metric defs), T06 (Home sections E/F/G containers), T04 (auth/role).

## Files Expected
- `src/pages/roster/PerformanceRosterPage.tsx`, `src/pages/roster/LeadSheetsPage.tsx`
- `src/features/home/sections/IndividualPerformance.tsx` (implement), `AppointmentsNoShows.tsx`
- `src/features/leads/list/leadPresets.ts` (drill-down presets), update Home Section D button wiring
- RPC additions documented in `src/lib/charts/RPC.md`
