tasks.description:
# Task 23 — Atty & AFF Settings (Manage Attorneys & Treatment Locations)

## Objective
Build the Atty & AFF Settings screen: two-tab management UI for attorney firms (`app2.attorneys`) and treatment locations (`app2.tx_locations`), each with searchable/paginated list, detail view, and edit form. This is the admin/senior-intake configuration surface for the reference data that powers the Intake Panel and Mapper.

## Context
React 18, mobile-first. Atty & AFF Settings (Screen 24, `/settings/atty-aff`) is gated to **admin + intake_supervisor** (IT Team + Senior Intake per ROLE-ACCESS-MATRIX). Two tabs: **Attorneys** (~33 pages) and **Locations** (~12 pages). **Attorneys:** list (firm name, status, emails, chevron) → detail (firm, ACTIVE/INACTIVE, Emails distribution list, States licensed) → edit (Email Targets required comma-separated, States required 50-state multi-select pills, Status toggle). **Locations:** list (AFF + COR, active + inactive) → detail (name, status, Type AFF/COR, Address, State, Int. EmailTargets, Ext. EmailTargets) → edit (Int./Ext. Email Targets, Address, State single-select, Status toggle; **Type is read-only**). Edits write to `app2.attorneys` / `app2.tx_locations` (RLS: writes require admin/intake_supervisor). Deferred workflows — no email automation; these targets are stored config used by later LD-email automation.

## Requirements
1. Build `AttyAffSettingsPage` (`/settings/atty-aff`), gated to admin + intake_supervisor (reuse `RequireRole`); two-tab layout (Attorneys | Locations).
2. **Attorneys tab:**
   - List: searchable, paginated (respect 1,000-row limit) from `app2.attorneys`; row shows firm name, status badge, emails, chevron.
   - Detail (`/settings/atty-aff/attorneys/:id`): firm, ACTIVE/INACTIVE, Emails (distribution list), States licensed; Edit button.
   - Edit: Email Targets (required, comma-separated, validated emails), States (required, 50-state multi-select pills), Status toggle (Active/Inactive); Cancel/Submit → update `app2.attorneys`.
3. **Locations tab:**
   - List: searchable, paginated from `app2.tx_locations` (AFF + COR, active + inactive); row shows name, status, type.
   - Detail (`/settings/atty-aff/locations/:id`): name, status, Type (AFF/COR), Address, State, Int. EmailTargets, Ext. EmailTargets; Edit button.
   - Edit: Int. Email Targets, Ext. Email Targets, Address, State (single-select), Status toggle; **Type read-only**; Cancel/Submit → update `app2.tx_locations`.
4. Validate email-target lists (comma-separated, well-formed); require States (attorneys) and the required location fields.
5. Optimistic update with rollback on error; success/error toasts.
6. Reuse the 50-state constant from T09; reuse multi-select pills + status toggle from the design system.
7. Enforce RLS — only authorized roles can write; others should not reach the page.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Page is gated to admin + intake_supervisor; both tabs render.
- [ ] Attorneys: list searches/paginates; detail shows all fields; edit validates Email Targets + States and persists to `app2.attorneys`.
- [ ] Locations: list searches/paginates; detail shows all fields; edit persists to `app2.tx_locations` with Type read-only.
- [ ] Email-target validation and required-field validation work.
- [ ] Edits reflect immediately (optimistic) with rollback on error.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

## Dependencies
- T02 (attorneys/tx_locations types), T03 (design system), T04 (auth/role guards), T05 (routes), T09 (50-state constant), T14 (tx_locations queries — soft).

## Files Expected
- `src/pages/settings/AttyAffSettingsPage.tsx`
- `src/features/settings/attorneys/*` (AttorneyList, AttorneyDetail, AttorneyEdit)
- `src/features/settings/locations/*` (LocationList, LocationDetail, LocationEdit)
- `src/lib/queries/attorneys.ts`, extend `src/lib/queries/txLocations.ts`

tasks.name: Capacitor iOS Integration
tasks.description:
# Task 24 — Capacitor iOS Integration + Simulator Build + In-App Notification Wiring

## Objective
Integrate Capacitor for iOS: add the iOS platform, configure native plugins (status bar, splash, keyboard, browser, haptics), wire the in-app notification center to native affordances, and build/run on the iOS Simulator. Lay the `device_tokens` plumbing for future APNs without enabling live push.

## Context
React 18 + Vite app wrapped with Capacitor for iPhone (A1). Per A6, **native APNs push is DEFERRED** (needs an Apple Developer account) — but the web app, iOS Simulator, and in-app notifications all work without it. This task adds the iOS platform (previously deferred from T01), syncs the web build into the native shell, configures essential native plugins, ensures the in-app notification center (T08) behaves well natively (badge, deep-links), and builds the `device_tokens` registration plumbing (table from SCHEMA.md) as a no-op/stub ready for Phase 2 — **no live push**. Mandatory: keep `node_modules/` and `ios/` ignored in git (T01).

## Requirements
1. Add the iOS platform: `npx cap add ios`; ensure `npm run build` + `npx cap sync ios` produce a runnable project. Add npm scripts (`cap:sync`, `ios:open`).
2. Install/configure core Capacitor plugins as needed: `@capacitor/status-bar`, `@capacitor/splash-screen`, `@capacitor/keyboard`, `@capacitor/app` (deep links/back), `@capacitor/browser` (open external Looker/Drive/Paylocity links from Profile/HR), `@capacitor/haptics`. Initialize them at app bootstrap.
3. Configure safe-area handling, status bar style (match theme), and splash screen with the brand.
4. Ensure `tel:` / `sms:` / `mailto:` links from Home, Lead Detail, etc. work in the native shell.
5. **In-app notification wiring:** ensure the T08 notification center deep-links work under Capacitor routing; optionally set the app icon badge to the unread count via a supported plugin (gracefully degrade if unavailable).
6. **device_tokens plumbing (stubbed):** implement a `registerDevice()` service that, when push is later enabled, would register an APNs token into `app2.device_tokens` (user_id, token, platform='ios'). For now it is a guarded no-op/stub (feature-flagged off) — build the data path but do not request push permission or send pushes.
7. Document the build/run steps in `ios/README.md` (or `docs/ios-build.md`): build web → cap sync → open in Xcode → run on Simulator; note the deferred APNs/Apple Developer requirement.
8. Verify the app boots and is navigable on the iOS Simulator.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] iOS platform added; `npm run build && npx cap sync ios` succeeds; project opens and runs on the Simulator.
- [ ] Core plugins (status bar, splash, keyboard, app, browser, haptics) configured and initialized.
- [ ] Safe-area, status bar, and splash render correctly; external links open via Browser plugin.
- [ ] `tel:`/`sms:`/`mailto:` work in the native shell.
- [ ] In-app notification deep-links work under Capacitor; badge updates if supported.
- [ ] `device_tokens` registration is stubbed/feature-flagged off (no live push, no permission prompt).
- [ ] iOS build/run steps documented; `ios/` remains git-ignored.

## Dependencies
- T01 (scaffold + capacitor.config), T05 (routing/shell), T07 (profile external links), T08 (notification center).

## Files Expected
- `ios/` (generated platform — git-ignored)
- `src/lib/native/*` (plugin bootstrap, `registerDevice` stub, badge helper)
- updated `capacitor.config.ts`, `package.json` scripts
- `docs/ios-build.md`

tasks.name: Polish + Bug Fixes + QA
tasks.description:
# Task 25 — Polish (Fix Carried-Over Bugs B1–B14, UX Improvements, QA Pass)

## Objective
Final polish pass: fix the catalogued carried-over bugs (B1–B14) from the current app, apply targeted UX improvements, and run a full QA sweep across all screens/roles. Ensure the app is consistent, accessible, and release-candidate quality.

## Context
React 18 + Capacitor app, mobile-first. The current app has a set of documented bugs (B1–B14) surfaced during the spec process; several have been flagged for fixing in their owning tasks, but this task is the catch-all to verify and close every one, plus general polish. Known bugs from SPEC: **B1** duplicate "Locked Down Today" widget on Dashboards (Screen 09); **B3** "Appointments & No SHow" capitalization typo; **B4** "intake Specialist" lowercase typo in filter; **B6** "Locked Down Droped" → "Dropped"; **B12** "Reimburseement" double-e typo (HR Screen 08); **B13** Submit Request popup stays open after submit (no auto-nav); plus the Profile bugs (inverted Compensation eye icon, wrong DOB/Start Date, "Logs(v2)" formatting), Intake Panel "Questionnare" typo and boolean "false"/"true" display, "Termed" duplicated directory filter, and data-quality/validation issues (E5). B2/B5/B7–B11/B14 are remaining catalogued items to confirm against the source bug list and resolve. Deferred workflows remain out of scope. This task does NOT add new features — it hardens what exists.

## Requirements
1. Compile the authoritative B1–B14 list (cross-reference SPEC + JON-REVIEW notes); for each, confirm whether already fixed in its owning task and verify, or fix here. Track status in a checklist file.
2. **Confirmed fixes to verify/apply:**
   - B1: ensure the Dashboards "Locked Down Today" widget renders once (no duplicate).
   - B3: "Appointments & No Show" (fix "SHow").
   - B4: "Intake Specialist" (capital I) in all filter modals.
   - B6: "Locked Down Dropped" (fix "Droped").
   - B12: "Submit Boost Reimbursement" (fix double-e) on HR hub.
   - B13: Submit Request auto-navigates on success (no stuck popup).
   - Profile: consistent eye-icon direction on all collapsible sections; correct DOB/Start Date; "Logs (v2)" label.
   - Intake Panel: "Questionnaire" spelling; booleans render Yes/No (never false/true).
   - Directory: "Termed" filter appears once.
3. Resolve remaining B-numbers (B2, B5, B7–B11, B14) per the source list; if any cannot be reproduced, document as "not reproduced / N/A" with rationale.
4. **UX improvements:** consistent loading skeletons and empty states across lists/charts; consistent error toasts; keyboard/safe-area behavior; tap-target sizing; consistent back-navigation; respect reduced-motion.
5. **Accessibility pass:** ARIA labels on icon buttons, focus management in modals/bottom-sheets, color-contrast check on teal/gray/red states.
6. **Cross-role QA sweep:** walk each primary screen as marketing_rep, marketing_leader, intake_specialist, intake_supervisor, admin; verify role gating, scoping, and that no unauthorized data leaks. Record results in a QA checklist.
7. Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and the production `npm run build`; resolve all errors/warnings introduced.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All B1–B14 items are either fixed-and-verified or documented as N/A with rationale, in a tracked checklist.
- [ ] The explicitly listed typos/display bugs (B1, B3, B4, B6, B12, B13, Profile, Intake Panel, Directory) are resolved.
- [ ] Consistent loading/empty/error states and accessibility improvements applied app-wide.
- [ ] Cross-role QA sweep completed for all 5 roles with documented results and no data-leak findings.
- [ ] `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run build` all pass clean.

## Dependencies
- All prior tasks (T01–T24) — this is the final integration/polish pass.

## Files Expected
- `build/QA-CHECKLIST.md`, `build/BUGS-B1-B14.md` (tracking)
- Targeted edits across existing feature files for each bug/UX/a11y fix (no new feature modules)
